title: Level 906.1
wikitext:
[[File:Darker Filmgrain.gif|2500px]]
{{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'serif'; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color:transparent; --theme-link-color: #FADB6F; --theme-page-text-color: #FAF9F6; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color:transparent; --theme-link-color: #FADB6F; --theme-page-text-color: #FAF9F6; } }} {{Blurbackground|file=The Sanctum.jpg|}} {{Blurquote|image=Old paper1.jpg||quote-text="I rather think that archives exist to keep things safe - but not secret."|person=Kevin Young.}} {{Class Custom Image|class=0|hexagon=🗄|bg-image=Paper for 906.1.jpg|safety=Safe|stability=Stable|entity=Docile Entities|text-color= #b38541}} '''Level 906.1''' is a sublevel between [[Level 906]] and Level 907 of [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. ''(Please play this for a better reading experience)'' {{Audio|align=middle|file=The Archive.mp3|volume=55|=true|loop=false}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 906.1: "The Arcadian Archive"}} {{Blurimage|file=1940's Backrooms Library.png|caption=A vintage photo taken from within the antechamber of Level 906.1 - Presumed to be taken by [[Entity 1|The Cameraman]]|align=left}} =='''Description'''== '''Level 906.1''', also known as '''''"The Arcadian Archive"''''', resembles an early-1940s library, filled with well kept bookshelves and filing cabinets, faint iridescent lamp lighting, and scattered files and papers of unknown origin. The interior of '''Level 906.1''' shifts only subtly and very rarely, so caution is advised for large parties traversing inside. The interior of '''Level 906.1''' is very snug and cozy, with fireplaces and sitting areas scattered about the shelves and cabinets. Soft off-white silk carpets cover the dark-oak hardwood flooring beneath. Beanbag chairs, sofas, and other miscellaneous lounge furniture fill the many rooms and corridors. Large portraits line the walls, depicting men and woman from all ages, with each of their faces being blurred as if water damaged. '''Level 906.1''' has been observed to play music ranging from the early 1920's to mid 1930's on rare occasions, consisting of old Vinyl Tracks that are reminiscent of music from '''[[The Frontrooms]]''' around the same era, though the tracks fade once the song is over. There is a recording of the phenomenon, however the video is corrupted, leaving only the audio track [audible now on this digital documentation]. {{Blurimage|file=The Sanctum.jpg|caption=A photo depicting '''906.1''''s "Sanctum" - Taken by Callen Marrow - '''''P.P.F.''''' '''''Chief'''''|align=right}} Besides the long halls and white carpet, several other styles of room exist within this level as well, such as the '''''"Sanctum"''''', coined by '''''P.P.F.''''' members in the local area. The Sanctum is a large, arched roofed hall with towering bookshelves and a Victorian era mural on the ceiling. Another section of '''Level 906.1''', known as the '''''"Antique Store"''''', is a tightly packed area full of winding corridors, vintage books, toys, photos, and memorabilia. It is advised against venturing into the '''''"Antique Store"''''', as it is incredibly easy to get lost inside it's endless rows. Reports from inside the '''''"Antique Store"''''' also consist of quote, "childhood nostalgia". Members of '''''P.P.F.''''' who have ventured inside and come back describe seeing, smelling, and feeling a sense of sentimentality. {{Blurimage|file=906.1 Corridor.jpg|caption=A photo depicting a narrow corridor within Level 906.1's ''"Antique Store"'' - Taken by '''''P.P.F. Member''''' Walton Barns|align=right}} =='''Entities'''== There are 2 main entities confirmed on this level, '''''"Archivists"''''' and '''''"Paperlings"'''''. Each is entirely harmless to travelers. ==='''Paperlings'''=== Small, origami-like creatures that scuttle between shelves, assembling themselves from discarded documents. They are harmless but curious, sometimes leading wanderers to significant discoveries, or dead ends. ===='''PAPERLING FACTS'''==== * '''''Paperlings''''', name coined by '''''P.P.F. Members''''', only inhabit Level 906.1, and other levels with extensive amounts of paper products. * Discovered on accident, when doused with water, '''''Paperlings''''' will become angry, and throw themselves into the person who got them wet over and over until they are completely dry, where they will go back to a docile and playful state. The repeated attacks of the '''''Paperling''''' are likely to do nothing, as they are very small and lack any strength to do any bodily harm. * '''''Paperlings''''', as described by '''''P.P.F. Member''''' Arline Cowell, quote:
"They act like angry little paper Chihuahuas, but become cute little friends when they cool off."
==='''Archivists'''=== Tall, black-skinned humanoids cloaked in long brown coats. They patrol this level frequently, appearing to organize and catalog items throughout the archive. They have deep sunken eyes, seemingly always in a saddened expression. '''''The Archivists''''' are mostly indifferent to wanderers, however they are known to give Water and Snacks to passers by, or if requested by a wanderer politely.
"These guys are great. They are the main reason we are able to stay here without starving to death. We practically have an infinite supply of nutrition because of our tall friends here." - Callen Marrow | '''''P.P.F. Chief'''''
=='''Colonies and Outposts'''== ==== '''''P.P.F. [Project Pillow Fort]''''' ==== '''''Project Pillow Fort''''', a small group of lost wanderers who set up a home in '''Level 906.1''', all joined together to keep themselves safe in the hellscape that is '''''[[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]'''''. Many of '''''P.P.F.''''''s members are seasoned veterans who've roamed and survived other levels for what feels like years, while a lucky few found the already banded group just days after they entered this nightmare.{{Blurquote|image=Old paper1.jpg||quote-text="We all happened upon eachother through other levels here and there, building up our little trecking team. We've lost people to this God forsaken place, but once we found this level, we knew it was home. Our home. Our safe haven from the unforgiving hell outside."|person=Callen Marrow | P.P.F. Chief }} =='''Due to Recent Events Beyond ''P.P.F.'' Control, Some Entrances/Exits are Prohibited'''== == '''Entrances''' == * Entering through a hollow circular bookcase on [[Level 906|'''''Level 906''''']] leads here. || Due to recent '''''AEG Anti-Entity''''' activities, this entrance has been '''''annexed'''''. * Reading the book titled '''''"The Archive of the Forgotten"''''' by '''''A.J. Wackwith''''' in [[The Endless Obscurity|'''''The Endless Obscurity''''']] leads here. * Following a white-silk carpet on misc. levels leads to a filing cabinet, which can be opened like a door, leading here. * Entering a trapdoor on the ceiling of [[The Basement|'''''The Basement''''']] leads here. * Entering through a library storefront on [[Level 524|'''''Level 524''''']] leads here. == '''Exits''' == *Exiting through the circular bookcase at the entrance of '''''Level 906.1''''' leads back to [[Level 906|'''''Level 906''''']]. || Due to recent '''''AEG Anti-Entity''''' activities, this exit has been '''''annexed'''''. *Falling asleep by a snowy window has a chance to go to either [[Level 505|'''''Level 505''''']], [[Level 386|'''''Level 386''''']], [[Level 385|'''''Level 385''''']], or [[Level 993|'''''Level 993''''']]. *Reading aloud the description of a level from inside any misc. document in '''Level 906.1''' leads to the described level. || '''NOTICE''': Exits via Text are Highly Prohibited, as Life Threatening Levels are '''''NOT''''' Uncommonly Documented || *Finding and exiting through trapdoors hidden under the silk carpet lead to [[The Basement|'''''The Basement''''']]. *Exiting through a rare library mall storefront leads to '''''[[Level 524]].''''' {{Redirect|Prev=906|Next=907}} ----- [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Sub-Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Safe]]
